meta.feed_title: World News Today: International News Headlines - The Hindu | The Hindu

items.title: Aid groups warn war in West Asia is hindering food, medicine from reaching millions
items.summary: Key pathways such as the Strait of Hormuz have been effectively shuttered and routes from strategic hubs such as Dubai, Doha and Abu Dhabi have also been impacted; transport costs have spiked with higher fuel and insurance rates, meaning less supplies can be delivered with the same amount of money.

items.title: Fuel reservoir hit at Russia’s Primorsk, NORSI refinery on fire after drone attacks
items.summary: Ukraine has stepped up attacks on Russian infrastructure targets in recent months

items.title: Iran-Israel war LIVE: Iran claims it downed C-130 aircraft and two Black Hawks during U.S. rescue mission
items.summary: Hezbollah on Sunday, April 5, said it had targeted an Israeli warship with a cruise missile; Trump claimed that two U.S. airmen were rescued in Iran after he set a deadline for a deal

items.title: Satellite firm Planet Labs to indefinitely withhold Iran war images
items.summary: California-based Planet Labs announced the decision in an email to customers and said the U.S. government had asked all ‌satellite imagery providers to indefinitely withhold images of the conflict region.

items.title: Spanish PM under fire as former top aide goes on graft trial
items.summary: Jose Luis Abalos and his former adviser Koldo Garcia are suspected of having pocketed kickbacks for handing out public contracts worth millions of euros for sanitary equipment during the COVID-19 pandemic

items.title: A long war in West Asia could take away support from Ukraine, says Zelenskyy
items.summary: Ukraine desperately needs more U.S.-made Patriot air defence systems to help it counter Russia’s daily barrages, Mr. Zelenskyy said, speaking to The Associated Press in an interview on April 4, in Istanbul.

items.title: Indonesia lays to rest peacekeepers killed in Lebanon
items.summary: The death of  Indonesian peacekeepers sparked calls from Indonesian authorities for an investigation and security guarantees for peacekeeping forces

items.title: Trump gives Iran 48 hours to make deal or face ‘Hell’
items.summary: U.S. President Donald Trump's latest threat came after a strike near an Iranian nuclear power plant prompted evacuations, with the Revolutionary Guards saying they hit a commercial ship in Bahrain allegedly linked to Israel

items.title: Afghan mother seeks justice after Pakistani bombing kills hundreds
items.summary: Afghanistan and Pakistan have been locked in an escalating conflict over claims from Islamabad that Kabul is harbouring militants responsible for cross-border attacks, which the Taliban government denies.

items.title: U.S. rescues airman whose F-15 was downed in Iran, say U.S. officials
items.summary: The U.S. President said the ‌United ⁠States Military ⁠pulled off one of the ‘most daring ​Search ‌and Rescue Operations in U.S. History’

items.title: For Trump, the cost of Iran war is rising
items.summary: At least six KC-135s were damaged in two Iranian missile and drone attacks at the Prince Sultan Air Base in Saudi Arabia; The U.S. has also lost at least 12 MQ-9 Reaper drones since the war began, according to some reports

items.title: Southern California wildfire mostly contained as officials lift many evacuation orders
items.summary: Encompassing roughly 16 square kilometres in Riverside County, about 103 kilometres east of Los Angeles, the Springs Fire was no longer growing and at least 75% contained Saturday (April 4), said Terra Fernandez, public safety information specialist for the Riverside County Fire Department.

items.title: South Korea asks Gulf nations for steady energy supply, safety of Korean vessels
items.summary: The ‌envoys said South Korea ​is a "top priority" nation and pledged to communicate closely with ⁠Seoul to ensure stable supply

items.title: Iran war starts pinching U.S.; gas prices, transport costs surge
items.summary: The average price of petrol in the U.S. has increased to $4.09 a gallon on Friday (April 3, 2026), up more than one dollar from just before the war and the highest level since August 2022

items.title: Kuwait power, water desalination plants damaged by Iranian attack: government
items.summary: Iran’s army said it was targeting “aluminum industries” in the UAE and U.S. military infrastructure in Kuwait, the official IRNA news agency reported

items.title: Iran FM Araghchi warns of contamination risk after U.S.-Israeli strikes on Bushehr nuclear power plant
items.summary: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi warned that continued attacks on the plant on Iran's southern coast could eventually lead to radioactive fallout that would "end life in GCC (Gulf Cooperation Council) capitals, not Tehran"; Bushehr is considerably closer to Kuwait, Bahrain and Qatar than it is to the Iranian capital.

items.title: Iran command rejects Trump's ‘helpless, nervous’ ultimatum
items.summary: General Ali Abdollahi Aliabadi, in a statement from the Khatam al-Anbiya Central Headquarters, said Mr. Trump's threat was a "a helpless, nervous, unbalanced and stupid action"

items.title: Mohammad Ishaq Dar | At the global high table
items.summary: A confidant of the Sharifs, the Foreign Minister is leading Pakistan’s diplomatic reboot, from restarting ties with Bangladesh to mediation in the Gulf

items.title: Trump administration files emergency motion to resume ballroom work, citing security issues
items.summary: Judge Richard Leon’s decision has left ‌the executive mansion “open and exposed” and is “threatening grave ‌national-security harms ⁠to the White House, the President and ⁠his family, and the President’s staff.”

items.title: Iran-Israel war updates: Trump says Iran military leaders killed in Tehran strike
items.summary: "Many of Iran's Military Leaders, who have led them poorly and unwisely, are terminated, along with much else, with this massive strike in Tehran!" U.S. President Donald Trump wrote on his Truth Social platform.

items.title: U.S. revokes green cards and visas of several Iranian nationals connected to Tehran government
items.summary: The latest actions were taken just this week when Secretary of State Marco Rubio determined they were no longer eligible for either lawful permanent resident status, or to enter the United States

items.title: Trump weighs broader cabinet shake-up as Iran war pressure grows
items.summary: Just 36% of Americans approve of U.S. President Donald Trump’s overall job performance, according to the latest Reuters/Ipsos survey, the lowest figure of his current term

items.title: Vehicle hits revelers during Lao New Year celebration in Louisiana
items.summary: Several people were injured when a vehicle struck revelers at a parade celebrating the Lao New Year on Saturday in rural Louisiana

items.title: Why does Trump want to pull out of NATO?
items.summary: Why does the U.S. administration believe that NATO’s founding principles and membership need to be ‘re-examined’? Why was NATO formed, and what are the recent developments that have led to fissures? Can the U.S. easily exit NATO? How much does it contribute to the alliance?

items.title: Ro Khanna | Exploring the new middle
items.summary: The Democratic congressman, who combines points from America First nationalism with the welfarism of the Democratic left, proposes state support for the development of manufacturing and the working class, and calls for moderation in maximalist tech capitalism

items.title: Relatives of Iran's late Gen. Qassem Soleimani arrested: U.S.
items.summary: U.S. revokes green cards and visas of several Iranian nationals connected to Tehran govt.

items.title: Explosion rocks Sudan’s capital; police blame war-era landmine
items.summary: Sudanese police said that the explosion occurred in the Burri neighbourhood in eastern Khartoum and was caused by a landmine, a remnant of the war, which detonated after “some residents set fire to waste” in the area

items.title: Three workers wounded in strike on North Rumaila oil field in southern Iraq
items.summary: The drone strikes ​had no impact on operations ⁠in this part of Iraq’s giant Rumaila oilfield, ‌as they targeted an equipment storage facility, said two Iraqi engineers working at the field

items.title: At least 12 persons, including eight of one family, killed in Afghan quake
items.summary: Official said five homes were destroyed and another 33 significantly damaged, affecting 40 families in the provinces of Kabul, Panjshir, Logar, Nangarhar, Laghman and Nuristan

items.title: Russia and Ukraine trade strikes as Zelenskyy goes to Turkiye
items.summary: Russia fired 286 drones at Ukraine overnight, 260 of which were downed, the Ukrainian Air Force said in a statement

items.title: Israel strikes south Lebanon’s Tyre after issuing evacuation warnings
items.summary: Tens of thousands flee Tyre due to evacuation orders, but about 20,000 residents persist, including 15,000 displaced from surrounding villages; attacks extend to Tyre’s port, Beirut’s southern suburbs

items.title: Explosion hits pro-Israel centre in the Netherlands
items.summary: The incident comes after a string of similar night-time attacks on Jewish sites in the Netherlands and neighbouring Belgium; Dutch police are investigating the incident

items.title: Iraq closes Shalamcheh crossing with Iran after airstrikes kill Iraqi
items.summary: Sources said at least ​five Iraqis ‌were seriously wounded in an airstrike, which hit ‌a passenger reception ​area on the Iranian side

items.title: IAEA says projectile hits near Iran's Bushehr nuclear plant, killing one
items.summary: “No ‌increase in radiation levels was reported,” the IAEA said

items.title: Tehran rejects 48-hour ceasefire proposal from U.S., says Iranian media
items.summary: The refusal highlights a continued hardening of Tehran’s position amidst the current regional situation

items.title: U.K. charges three men with arson over attack on Jewish community ambulances
items.summary: The ambulances ‌were set on fire on March 23 ‌in what British Prime Minister ‌Keir ⁠Starmer described as a “deeply ⁠shocking antisemitic arson attack”.

items.title: Three Greek Ministers quit as EU investigates alleged farm subsidy fraud
items.summary: The European Public Prosecutor’s Office is seeking immunity waivers for 11 lawmakers in a case that has fuelled public anger in Greece and raised concerns in the farming sector

items.title: Pakistan rejects media reports of collapse of initiative to facilitate U.S., Iran talks
items.summary: Speculation emerged after a senior official of the Foreign Ministry briefed a select group of journalists on Pakistan’s efforts to find a negotiated settlement of the ongoing conflict

items.title: One killed, dozens hurt as football stadium event in Peru goes awry
items.summary: 60 injured during a “flag-waving event” ‌popularly known as Matute at the Alejandro Villanueva Stadium in the Peruvian capital

items.title: Fast-growing wildfire in Southern California triggers evacuations
items.summary: A Cal Fire map showed the fire burning in a recreational area near the city of Moreno Valley, which has a population of roughly 200,000. The city is 103 km east of Los Angeles.

items.title: Forget democracy, says Burkina Faso military leader Traore
items.summary: Captain Ibrahim Traoré aims to rule ⁠for long term, says ‘democracy kills’

items.title: Russia made nearly no territorial gains in Ukraine in March: Analysis
items.summary: Across the entire front line, the Russian army seized only 23 sq. km in March, losing territory in some areas

items.title: China moves to regulate digital humans, bans addictive services for children
items.summary: The new rules aim to fill a ‌gap in governance in the digital human sector, setting clear ​red lines for the healthy development of the industry, according to an analysis

items.title: Downed planes spell new peril for Trump as Tehran hunts missing U.S. pilot
items.summary: The prospect of a U.S. service person alive and on the run in Iran raises the stakes for Washington in a conflict with low public support among Americans ‌and no sign of an imminent end

items.title: Collapse of Tokyo's aging cherry blossom trees during viewing season raises safety concerns
items.summary: Last year, 85 trees fell in Tokyo parks, injuring three people, and many were cherry trees, according to Masakazu Noguchi, a Tokyo metropolitan official in charge of public parks

items.title: Two U.S. aircraft shot down as war in Iran escalates; at least one crew member is missing
items.summary: Separately, Iranian state media said a US. A-10 attack aircraft crashed in the Persian Gulf after being struck by Iranian defence forces

items.title: Iran executes two linked to Opposition group: reports
items.summary: The group said ‌the two men executed ​on Saturday (April 4) were arrested in January ⁠2024 and had their ⁠death sentences upheld in December 2025.

items.title: Drone, missile strike kills one in Russia’s Rostov: Governor
items.summary: Falling drone debris also hit a foreign-flagged cargo vessel in the Sea of Azov, causing a fire, while air defences destroyed drones over Taganrog Bay and other districts, the Governor said

items.title: WHO warns about attacks on Iran health facilities, regional threat
items.summary: The WHO cited 116 verified attacks on healthcare in the countries concerned and reported that "the escalating crisis is sharply increasing the risk of communicable disease outbreaks”

items.title: Bahrain cracks down on dissent as Iran war reignites internal unrest
items.summary: Bahrain, a monarchy that hosts the U.S. Navy’s 5th Fleet, has arrested dozens of people throughout the war for filming strikes and demonstrations, expressing support for Iran, and on suspicion of spying for it.

items.title: Lebanon’s displaced Shia Muslims face rising hostility as airstrikes fuel fear and evictions
items.summary: Sectarian tensions are a sensitive issue in Lebanon because the country fought a 15-year civil war ending in 1990 that largely broke down along sectarian lines

items.title: Three peacekeepers wounded in Lebanon are Indonesian: U.N.
items.summary: U.N. Information Centre (UNIC) in Jakarta said the "origin of the explosion" was unknown, but identified the wounded peacekeepers as Indonesian

items.title: Bangladesh cuts office hours, turns off wedding lights to save energy
items.summary: Bangladesh imports 95% of its oil and gas needs, mostly from West Asia and said that, while there are adequate stocks of petrol and diesel, it hopes to mitigate the effects of "unsecured" supply lines

items.title: Tamil Nadu-born Rini Sampath in race to become Washington DC Mayor
items.summary: Others in the mayoral race are Janeese Lewis George, Kenyan McDuffie, Gary Goodweather, Robert L Gross and Rhonda Hamilton

items.title: Does Iran have a path to the bomb?
items.summary: Iran is a nuclear-capable state; in the event Israel strikes it with a nuclear weapon, it could retaliate – but how quickly is a separate question

items.title: Pakistan government slashes petrol price by PKR 80 per litre, day after sharp hike met severe backlash
items.summary: The government on Thursday (April 2) announced an unprecedented hike of 43% and 55% in the prices of petrol and high-speed diesel (HSD), respectively

items.title: Israel says striking Hezbollah sites in Beirut after destroying bridge
items.summary: A journalist heard two loud explosions in the capital within half an hour and saw smoke billowing from one of them

items.title: Up to 75 women expected to benefit from Bank of America settlement in Epstein sex abuse, say lawyers
items.summary: Lawyers for women abused by Epstein had sued the bank, saying it ignored suspicious financial transactions involving Epstein that occurred while he was abusing girls and women from June 2008 to his arrest in early July 2019 on federal sex trafficking charges.

items.title: Bahrain postpones vote on UN resolution aimed at ending Iran's stranglehold on Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: The diplomats, speaking on condition of anonymity because council consultations have been private, said the vote will now be held sometime next week

items.title: Iran-Israel war updates: Iran military says downed second U.S. jet in Gulf
items.summary: The total year-on-year increase in Pentagon spending would be the largest since World War II, U.S. media reported, although presidential budgets are wish lists that have to be approved by Congress, rather than binding orders.
